registry.description: Indignation is the righteous anger that arises in response to genuine injustice or wrong — the heated moral reaction to what ought not to be. The Hebrew vocabulary distinguishes between indignation as an inner boiling (za'am) and the scorching heat of displeasure. It is regularly attributed to God in response to wickedness and idolatry. Indignation is not the same as personal anger at being hurt: it is anger on behalf of what is right, which gives it a moral character that personal anger does not always have.
